title: Develop a Database Connector Incrementally
text: Develop a Database Connector Incrementally During connector development, you can integrate an incomplete DBMS connector with Uniface for testing purposes. Note: Uniface does not guarantee the correct operation of products integrated with an incomplete database connector. To develop a connector incrementally: Run the Uniface installation program in the Uniface installation directory and select the user-defined connector. For more information, see the relevant information for your connector guide. In the Uniface IDE, develop a test application with an entity in the database for which you are developing the connector. Implement the Info('I') , Message ('M') , and Logon ('L') connector requests. For more information, see Info ('I') , Message ('M') and Logon ('L') . Define a connector error code for every connector function that is not implemented, as follows: Set uctrl->udrverr to the connector error code. Return to Uniface with the return value for Exceptional error. Compile the incomplete connector. Run the test application with the /pri command line setting set to 255. Attempt to enter, store, modify and retrieve data. For more information, see I/O Message Levels . Observe the error messages in the message frame. If an error message appears for a connector request that is not implemented, the integration of the connector has been successful. After running the test application, implement the first connector function that returned an exceptional error. Compile the connector. Repeat this procedure from the step 6, continuing the loop until you have successfully implemented all connector requests. Related Links Integrate a User-Defined Database Connector with Uniface Uniface allows you to define up to ten user-defined DBMS connectors, called UD0 through UD9, in every installation of Uniface or the Uniface Server.
